macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yosei's Position on Pregnant and Nursing Women: Three Months Without Bleeding

micro_segments.title: Introduction to mesuleket damim categories and Rabbi Yosei's requirement of 90 days
The baraita of Rabbi Yosei before Rabbi Eliezer and the singular/dual language debate
Combining pregnancy and nursing days to reach 90 days: cases and examples
Detailed baraita on how pregnancy and nursing periods combine toward three months

macro_segments.title: The Problem of Bleeding During Childbirth: How to Count Uninterrupted Periods

micro_segments.title: The gemara's difficulty: how can three months be uninterrupted if bleeding occurs at birth?
Proposed solutions: dry birth (yavesha) and distinction between dam niddah and dam leidah
Deep analysis of how childbirth bleeding differs from menstrual bleeding conceptually
Extended discussion of defining menstrual blood (dam niddah) and its practical implications for fibroids and wounds

macro_segments.title: When Bleeding Changes Status: One Bleed vs. Multiple Bleeds by Category

micro_segments.title: Tannaic dispute: does one bleeding establish that future bleeds will recur?
Why pregnant and nursing differ from young and menopausal: static vs. flux states
Amoraim debate (Rav, Shmuel, Rabbi Yochanan, Reish Lakish) on which categories change after first bleed

macro_segments.title: External Causes of Bleeding and Establishing a Veset (Regular Cycle)

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: when external force (ones) causes bleeding, it doesn't change mesuleket damim status but woman becomes niddah
Example of calisthenics causing breakthrough bleeding and establishing a veset
Baraita: ones cannot establish a veset even if it happens multiple times
Can a veset be based on days alone or calisthenics alone, or must it combine both factors?

macro_segments.title: Women Who Must Check: Frequency, Exceptions, and Special Cases

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: all mesuleket damim women must check regularly despite no expected bleeding
Exceptions: niddah and women in post-partum tahara period do not need to check
Frequency of checking: twice daily (morning and twilight) and before sexual relations
Stringencies for kohanot (wives/daughters of priests) handling terumah

macro_segments.title: Can a Woman Establish a Veset During Her Niddah Days?

micro_segments.title: Dispute between Reish Lakish and Rabbi Yochanan on whether later niddah days establish a pattern
Closed well vs. open well: when niddah bleeding is relevant for establishing a veset

topical_tags.term: dam niddah vs. dam leidah (menstrual vs. childbirth blood)
Rav, Shmuel, Rabbi Yochanan, Reish Lakish (on bleeding recurrence)
Reish Lakish on veset in zivah days
Rabbi Yochanan on veset in niddah days
